visual_checks: Review each slide in the presentation and check whether it has a visible or programmatic title
Compare slide titles across the entire presentation to identify duplicates
Check if any slide titles are positioned off-slide or hidden from view while still present in the structure
Verify that each slide title is descriptive and distinguishes the slide from others in the presentation
Look for slides where the title placeholder has been deleted or left empty
pass_conditions: Every slide in the presentation has a unique, descriptive title
Slide titles accurately reflect the content of each slide
Titles are programmatically associated with the slide (using the title placeholder or marked as the slide title)
If a title is visually hidden for design purposes, it is still present in the slide structure as an accessible title
fail_conditions: One or more slides have no title at all (title placeholder deleted or empty)
Multiple slides share the same title text, making them indistinguishable for assistive technology users
A slide title is dragged off the visible slide area to hide it rather than using proper hide-title techniques
Slide titles are generic and non-descriptive (e.g., 'Slide 1', 'Slide 2', 'Continued')
A text box is used as a visual title but is not set as the actual slide title element in the slide structure
na_conditions: The document is not a presentation format (not PowerPoint, Google Slides, or similar)
ne_conditions: The presentation structure could not be parsed to determine slide titles
Slides failed to render so their titles cannot be verified
common_mistakes: Deleting the title placeholder and using a regular text box for the visual heading, which is not recognized as the slide title
Using the same title on continuation slides (e.g., 'Budget Overview' on three consecutive slides) instead of differentiating them (e.g., 'Budget Overview - Revenue', 'Budget Overview - Expenses')
Dragging the title placeholder off the visible slide area instead of using the 'Hide Title' accessibility technique
Leaving auto-generated titles like 'Title and Content' or 'Blank' from the slide layout
Having a title on the master slide but not on individual slides

examples.pass: A 15-slide presentation has a unique descriptive title on each slide, such as 'Q3 Revenue Summary', 'Regional Sales Breakdown', and 'Projected Growth for 2026'
examples.fail: A presentation has 5 slides with no title placeholder and 3 slides all titled 'Overview', making navigation impossible for screen reader users
examples.partial: Most slides have unique titles but two slides share the title 'Summary' and one slide has its title placeholder dragged off the visible area
